danger_signals.visual_form: animated sprite characters moving toward player
danger_signals.meaning: active hostile combatants requiring engagement or avoidance
heavily-armed threats with superior firepower; player must use vehicles or grenades
ranged threats from above; indicate aerial assault phase

danger_signals.visual_form: red spot appearing below player character on screen
danger_signals.meaning: computer-controlled suppressive fire; player is stalling and must advance upward

danger_signals.visual_form: vehicle sprite damage state or on-screen fuel indicator
danger_signals.meaning: commandeered tank losing protection; player must exit before explosion

safety_signals.visual_form: collectible sprites distributed along level path
safety_signals.meaning: temporary advantage or weapon enhancement; indicates safe passage zones

safety_signals.visual_form: player sprite entering enemy tank sprite
safety_signals.meaning: temporary invulnerability from enemy bullets; shift to vehicle combat mode
mobile sanctuary with flight capability; enables water crossing and area control

safety_signals.visual_form: camera scroll moving toward village of Ikari at top of screen
safety_signals.meaning: advancement toward objective; mission progress indicator

faction_indicators.color_identity: red (Colonel Ralf Jones) / blue (Second Lieutenant Clark Still)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player sprites armed with machine gun (right hand) and grenades (left hand); visible weapon usage asymmetry

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: swastika present in final room, indicating neo-Nazi enemies
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: soldier sprites, tank sprites, helicopter sprites; no color differentiation documented
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: progression toward hostile village; enemy concentration increases toward objective
General Kawasaki (JP version) / Colonel Cook (US/EU version) positioned at village of Ikari; final room location signals rescue objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: sparse enemy sprite density; open movement space visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies advance methodically; no tracking fire yet

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: increased enemy sprite count; tank and helicopter appearances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must commandeer vehicles; call-for-fire tracking begins if stalling detected

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: red spot appears below player character on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: computer AI forces upward progression through suppressive fire; mandatory screen scroll

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player sprite integrated into tank or helicopter sprite; weapon configuration changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player gains temporary invulnerability to bullets; fuel/ammo management required; vehicle damage possible from explosions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: swastika symbol visible in final room; village of Ikari at screen top occupied by enemy forces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: intense enemy concentration; rescue target (named character) positioned for liberation

environmental_storytelling.example: village of Ikari positioned at top of screen; player must traverse upward through enemy territory to reach it; geographic direction reinforces narrative journey
swastika symbol appears in final room only; late-game reveal of enemy identity as neo-Nazi force; contextualizes military opposition thematically
red sprite (Ralf) vs. blue sprite (Clark) in two-player mode; color identity persists throughout mission; visual distinction enables player differentiation
tanks and helicopters distributed strategically along path; player must commandeer enemy vehicles; suggests enemy superior technology, requiring tactical acquisition
General Kawasaki (JP) / Colonel Cook (US/EU) named as hostage at Ikari; named character creates personal stakes; village destination becomes human liberation mission

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: eight-directional movement via joystick push; independent rotary aiming via joystick rotation; enables forward movement with lateral or rear fire
machine gun fires from right hand; grenades thrown from left hand; no shot fires directly ahead; requires aiming rotation for full firing arc
advance toward player; basic attack pattern; no documented evasion AI
occupies terrain; turret rotates to track player; bullets bypass grenades but not vehicle occupancy
bullet_immunity active; fuel management required; vehicle destruction triggers player damage unless exit executed; limited fuel supply creates time pressure
initiates call-for-fire tracking (red spot appears); forces upward screen progression through suppressive fire; prevents passive play strategy

notes: Ikari Warriors NES communicates world-state primarily through sprite density, vehicle availability, and call-for-fire mechanics rather than environmental color shifts. The asymmetric weapon system (gun right, grenades left) creates constant aiming pressure. Neo-Nazi symbolism (swastika in final room) functions as late-game faction revelation. Character color coding (red/blue) enables cooperative identity without dialogue. The game's core manifestation tension exists between player freedom (rotary aiming, eight-direction movement) and forced progression (call-for-fire tracking). Vehicle mechanics shift threat model mid-game: bullets become irrelevant, fuel becomes critical. No documented faction-specific environmental markers exist beyond the final room Nazi symbol.
